programme.desc: Buckle up with Humberside Police as they chase down dodgy drivers and put the most dangerous people on our roads behind bars. Police get a call from a petrol station reporting a driver has refused to pay for fuel, got aggressive with staff and then spun off. PC Billy Stead and Nathan Saltmer race to intercept the car. On the A63 in Hull, PC Luke Thundercliffe has his eyes on a white van driver with his foot down. When he pulls it over, the driver says it's a work van and he's insured to drive it under work policy. The only problem is the driver isn't showing on the policy and to make matters worse he doesn't even have a full licence. And PC Martin Leak is on patrol in Hull when he watches a car drive straight through a pedestrian crossing, almost hitting someone trying to cross. Narrated by Nicky Campbell. Series Prod: Johnny Perks; Exec Prods: Zoë Callan/Dympna Jackson; Dirs: Sam Knowles/John Nichols/Phil Hirst/Emma Fraser; Purple Productions (S9 Ep1)

programme.title: Love Nature
programme.desc: Glass pebbles are what makes Seaham Beach unusual. Victorian glass factories used to throw their rejects straight in the sea and in one of the most effective examples of nature's recycling, the sea now washes them up as glass pebbles. Volunteer Caitlin Elwin explains the history of sea glass on Seaham beach; strikingly beautiful glass pebbles that have been transformed by the sea over decades from the refuse of Victorian glass factories. Contradictorily seen as both desirable and as litter, visitors are encouraged to collect sea glass to help 'clean' the coast. Exec Prod: Charlie Bunce; Curve Media (S1 Ep3)

programme.title: The Motorway
programme.desc: The motorway patrollers deal with a pack of escaped sheep, and a driver attempting to get home on an empty tank is confronted when he runs out of fuel in a live lane of the motorway.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Vernon is struggling with unexplained stomach pain, Jack is experiencing worrying chest pains following brain surgery and Dr Libby conducts a mental health review with Sheyenne.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A global internet outage causes systems to go down, so the team must go back to basics. Jeanie, 92, is brought into casualty for the second time this week. She previously had a fall, but today she's having difficulty breathing. Louise, 36, slipped over and fractured her ankle. Following an x-ray, she's taken to resus for assessment. Her leg needs correcting quickly to restore her blood flow. And in paediatrics, Henry, eight, has fallen off a climbing and shattered his front teeth. Series Prod: Kayleigh Smith; Prod/Dir: Philip Hirst/Anna Evans/Steph Brunzel-Roberts; Series Dir: Joe Lister; Exec Prods: Kerry Brierley/Elaine Hackett; Crackit Productions Limited (S11 Ep5)
In Barnsley's Casualty department, it's a battle of life and death every single day.

programme.title: Surgeons: A Matter of Life or Death
programme.desc: Leo (17) faces a life-threatening crisis - his liver is failing and he needs an urgent transplant. Since he was a baby, Leo has suffered from biliary atresia - a condition that is now causing cirrhosis of his liver. His body is increasingly unable to cope. The transplant is a hugely risky procedure. One wrong move could mean his patient could lose litres of blood within seconds on the table. And to make matters more challenging, Leo has an unusually small hepatic artery and portal vein - making an already challenging operation even riskier. Across the hospital, Daniel (47) also faces huge surgery. After a routine procedure revealed that he had a major cancer in his right kidney, further investigation found that that the cancer had grown into the renal vein and into a major vessel in his heart. If left untreated - Daniel lives with the very real threat that part of his tumour could break off and make its way to his lungs - causing sudden death. Series Prods: Sue Learoyd/Lizzie Faulkner; Prods/Dirs: Luke Farrell/Alex Gower-Jackson; Exec Prod: Malcolm Brinkworth; Brinkworth Productions Ltd (S3 Ep5/6)
Remarkable life-saving operations and extraordinary stories of surgical skill.

programme.title: Love Nature
programme.desc: Three hundred years ago Red Kites were a thriving species in Northern Ireland but due to disruption of their precious habitats, the bird went extinct from the island of Ireland. Fifteen years ago, conservationists reintroduced the species to the province. Doctor Eimear Rooney, Northern Ireland Raptor Study Group (NIRSG), takes us to witness one such burgeoning flock's nightly routine, roosting together in a County Down valley. Eimear and several other birdwatchers regularly track and monitor the Red Kites' re-introduction and integration into the Northern Irish ecosystem. Exec Prods: Fintan Maguire/Catriona Doyle; High Rock Media (S1 Ep4)
Short films focussing on the people, places and projects protecting and restoring nature.

programme.title: The Motorway
programme.desc: Gavin and Ryan deal with a vehicle that's smashed into the central reservation of the M62. Vicky and Steve receive reports of a badger crossing the Manchester ship canal via the M6.

programme.title: The Underground Map of Britain
programme.desc: An entertaining, fact-fuelled ride through London's tube network, revealing the history of the world's first, and most iconic, underground railway system, which today carries 1.3 billion passengers a year.
An entertaining ride through London's tube network, revealing its deep history.

programme.title: Spain with Michael Portillo
programme.desc: Michael travels to Mallorca, starting in the capital Palma, heading to the top of its Gothic Cathedral. From there he goes to the north, once almost cut off from the rest of Mallorca by the towering Tramontana Mountains. With the help of a new road tunnel, Michael reveals how the beautiful town of Soller made its fortune from selling citrus fruit to the French during the 19th and 20th centuries. Here he rides Soller's vintage tram that originally transported oranges to the nearby port. He savours a Mallorcan open sandwich called pamboli and hears of Hollywood stars who visited in the 1970s. The next day Michael visits a working almond farm to help harvest one of Mallorca's most treasured exports. From there he returns to Palma and a bakery and makes an ensaimada, a traditional pastry with roots in the island's historic Jewish community, before a day at the races, where he has a flutter on a local form of horse racing called trotting. Prod/Dir: Dave Minchin; Exec Prod: Charlie Bunce; Curve Media Limited (Ep5)
Join Michael on a journey of discovery deep into his secret Spain off the tourist trail.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben visits northern England for a journey into a different kind of wild living - 'continuous cruising' on a narrowboat - staying onboard with his host Dave, whose move to an off-grid life on the Cheshire canals became a lifeline after personal tragedy. Ben discovers how the inland waterways offered Dave not just solace, but a renewed sense of purpose, introducing him to the rhythms of living afloat and to the extraordinary wildlife the canals support, which has helped shape his new way of life. The pair experience the contrast between a slower pace of life and the physical demands of boat living. As Ben takes on boat maintenance and adapts to the challenges of the waterways, he comes to appreciate how this unique journey through nature has helped restore his host. Exec Prod: Natalie Wilkinson; Dir: Natalie Wilkinson; Rushlight Films (S21 Ep4)
Ben Fogle travels round the world to visit people who have given up the rat race.

programme.title: Jewels of the Med
programme.desc: Hvar is the longest and sunniest island in Croatia. Home to over 10,000 residents, known for its fields of lavender, rare and remarkable lace making, its secluded bays with beautiful beaches and six Unesco heritage listings. It's an island associated with luxury and beauty, a magnet for the rich and the famous. One of the island's most glamorous hangouts can be found in the capital, owned by Ivan Gospodnetic, and renowned for its seafood. Each day Ivan sends out a fleet of seven fishing boats. In 1868, Hvar became the birthplace of organised tourism in Europe which saw an explosion in popularity in the 1960s thanks to The Hvar Hygienic Society, a group who promoted the island as a health retreat. The Hvar Summer Festival is one of the oldest summer festivals on the Adriatic coast. This year marks the first time Jelena oversees the running of the festival's programme. Narrated by Joanna Lumley. Exec Prods: Dympna Jackson/ Melanie Darlaston; Purple Productions/GroupM Motion Entertainment Limited
Joanna Lumley narrates this exploration of Mediterranean islands, cuisine and culture.

programme.title: Jane McDonald: From Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Jane reaches her final destination, deep in the Arctic Circle, exploring the most northerly inhabited archipelago in the world, Svalbard, a remote and rugged wilderness where Jane kayaks in icy fjords, encounters polar bears and Atlantic walruses in their natural habitat and visits the longest glacier in the northern hemisphere. Exec Prods: Kieron Collins/Mark Powell; Dir: Jenny MacLeod; JetSetTV Ltd (Ep6/6)
